BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin on the Saturday painting class after the Dearborn Community Arts Center mentioned studio-room fees might rise. Pre-review for David Chen (CPA).

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Class side income, not the dental practice. Need to revisit with David once 2025 actuals are closed.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~26-28 students at current cost structure. At 22 students plus drop-ins and commission sales we are roughly at break-even; the class is more about teaching than margin. Main concern is the summer dip.
ActionItems: Wait for the Dearborn Community Arts Center to confirm the studio-room fee situation
Revisit after year-end with David Chen for proper modeling
